PERSONAL TRAINING CLIENT INTAKE FORM
You can use either the imperial or metric system to fill in this form. Just

Strength level

• Bench press

• Squat (excl. body

weight)

MennoHenselmans.com

• Overhead press
With dumbells of 55 lbs x 6

Goals
What are your fitness goals?
building muscle, I want to look bigger but as lean as possible, shredded
physique can be put in second importance, as I really want to have
more volume on all my body.

Do you have any muscle groups you do nót want to get bigger?
I want to get bigger all muscles.

Will you be performing any other form of exercise alongside
strength training (e.g. soccer, yoga, Olympic Weightlifting)?

no

Which of the following options best describes your dedication
level?
A: Sustainability is most important to me. As long as I'm moving in the
right direction, I don't care about the rate of progress.
B: I want to strike a reasonable balance between good results in
proportion to effort and sustainability.
C: I will do whatever it takes to achieve maximum results without
compromising my health.

pages.text:
MennoHenselmans.com

C: I will do whatever it takes to achieve maximum results without
compromising my health

How often in a week would you be prepared to train for maximal
results?
I am looking to train 4 times a week, if it is recommended to build
muscle in a short/medium time, I want to start seeing results and
changes in my body in the shortest time possible.

Misc.
What is your occupation?
You may be general here, e.g. student/manager/personal trainer/manual
labor/doctor. The aim is to characterize your circadian rhythm, stress and
activity level.
Desk job

Please list any pathologies and injuries (disabilities, allergies,
illnesses, syndromes, disorders etc.) you have or had.
None.

Do you insist on any special diet (e.g. ketogenic, vegan)?
no

MennoHenselmans.com

• How much did you weigh before you started
lifting and what was your height at that point?
58kg , 171cm
• Were you heavy as a baby?
NO

Please attach a picture to this form in which the back of your right hand is
clearly visible: place your right hand on a flat surface with your fingers
touching each other and then take a picture of it.

Current Program
Please describe or attach your current diet plan in detail.

Meal 1 - protein shake with banana and oats zero sugar
Meal 2 - white eggs with vegetables + 2 pieces of white bread + 1 cup
of fruit
Meal 3 - 1 cup of rice + 200g of chicken breast + 1 cup of salad ½
avocado
Meal 4 - 2 cups of rice + 200g of meat + 1 cup of salad + ½ avocado
Meal 5 - 2 pieces of rice cakes with 1 spoon of almond butter
Meal 6 - 200g of salmon + 2 tortillas + ½ avocado + salad

Please describe or attach your current training program in detail.

MennoHenselmans.com

Bro split

Please return completed form to Coaching@MennoHenselmans.com and
attach at least two full-body pictures of yourself in clear lighting (without
excessive shadows; not your most flattering Facebook selfies), preferably
including a back double biceps and front relaxed pose.
If you recently had blood work done, please attach its results as well.

MennoHenselmans.com

Designing Marco's diet
Here we have an intermediate+ level 'bodybuilder' that wants to do a lean
bulk with a training frequency of 4x/wk. His strength level is only
intermediate, but this body composition is clearly advanced, so for the
sake of his energy surplus, I've given him a 3% energy surplus with a
little extra on consecutive training days (see below).

PAL: Desk job so 1.

TEF: He's dedicated so 20%.

With a BW of 76 kg and 8% BF based on his InBody scan, that gives him a
BMR of 1880 and a maintenance intake of 2569.

Protein intake: I've added 2 to this bodyweight since he'll get there
quickly during the bulk and then multiplied this by 1.8 to get 140 g
protein per day on average.

Fat intake: I've gone with 30% of total energy intake here, i.e. 88 g per
day on average. Since he'll have plenty of calories to fill in, I've added a
note this is the minimum and he's free to increase this up to 45% of
energy intake.

Carbohydrates fill in the remainder, i.e. 322 g per day on average.

Nutrient timing: He trains 4x per week and he's quite advanced, so if he
would train in the mornings, I might have gone with a diet set-up with 4
bulk days and 3 cut/PSMF. However, training in the evening is preferable,
which leaves time for only a single meal followed by the overnight fast.
That means firstly that we want an early morning meal and thus a meal
frequency of at least 4 without morning IF. Secondly, his anabolic window

MennoHenselmans.com

will extend well into the next day. The majority of his anabolic window will
actually take place on his rest days. Thus, I've gone for a simple constant
daily energy intake.

I've also distributed his fat intake equally over time, as fat has no timing
requirement and there's no need for major calorie cycling here.

For carbohydrate and protein timing, things get a bit more complex. On
his first training days, I've allocated double the protein and carbohydrate
intake to his last post-workout meal of the day, as this will be his only
meal in the anabolic window (easily calculated by multiplying the daily
total by 1/5th for meals 1-4 and 2/5th for meal 4).
Then on the next rest day I've gone with 4 square meals a day, as this
whole day will likely fall in his anabolic window with his high training
volume per session.
Once a week we'll have a consecutive training day. In this case I've gone
for the higher rest day macros for meals 1-3 and the higher post-workout
meal macros from his training day. Note that for simplicity's sake, you
could make all training days the same and it wouldn't make much of a
difference in his results.
Lastly, I've added PSMF instructions in case he ever gets a consecutive
rest day.

See the macro overview I gave him below.
